reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: worker.js loads a REAL transformers.js translation pipeline (pipeline('translation', 'Xenova/m2m100_418M', {device:'wasm', dtype:'q8'})) and calls pipe(text, {src_lang, tgt_lang, num_beams, max_new_tokens:256}) on-device — one multilingual model translating directly between any of 100 languages with NO English pivot. Streaming via TextStreamer gives the real greedy decode cadence. Conformance calls-real-inference (source) + loads-clean (console-clean) tested pass; io-shape-semantic manual-evidenced. No measured latency fabricated.
rubric.notes: src_lang/tgt_lang are passed through to the real M2M100 pipeline, so the direct any→any behaviour is genuine, not an English-pivot fake.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = translation_text. worker.js returns {translation, srcLang, tgtLang, inTokens, outTokens, intervals, beams, ms} — the real input_ids length, the streamed output, and per-token intervals for greedy decode. io-shape-semantic conformance assertion covers a real translation in the chosen target language.
rubric.notes: Per-token intervals are empty under beam search (streamer skipped) — disclosed, honest.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares Xenova/m2m100_418M (linked, 5 occurrences), the translation task, q8 dtype, WASM backend, ~470 MB and MIT license — matching models.json. declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation conformance assertions tested pass.
rubric.notes: M2M100 418M: direct many-to-many across 100 languages. Inference off the main thread.

rubric.evidence: The page uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init (modelId Xenova/m2m100_418M, q8, ~470 MB, requiresWebGPU:false); a valid current cached copy auto-initialises with no Load click. shared-auto-init-loader conformance assertion tested pass.
rubric.notes: ~470 MB is a large download; the never-silently-re-download guarantee + Clear-cache control are important and the size is surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: runStatus role=status aria-live; streamed partial translation in an aria-live region; worker errors surface on the page; shared loader adds checking→initialising→ready + Retry. live-status-region + errors-on-the-page tested pass; honest-progress-retry-offline / runs-without-webgpu-fallback manual-evidenced.

Real, labelled controls: the source text <textarea>, a Source-language <select> and a Target-language <select> (id src/tgt), a Beams range slider, and a translate button. All keyboard-operable. controls-operable conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The two language pickers are the heart of the demo — they map to the real src_lang/tgt_lang M2M100 args, so the user directly drives the model's direct-translation routing.

rubric.evidence: No canned translation: streamed text, token counts and per-token intervals are all from the real pipeline. no-fake-output conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.

translate button + language selects carry names (buttons-have-names tested pass); status role=status aria-live; output region aria-live; selects labelled; page lang=en; WCAG-AA light+dark. images-have-alt tested pass.
rubric.notes: The See-inside token/interval information is text, not canvas — available to assistive tech. Note: the translated output region carries the page lang=en rather than a dynamic lang matching the target language (open item — a dynamic lang attribute would improve screen-reader pronunciation).

rubric.evidence: responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) conformance assertions present; design-system panels + flex-wrap language pickers. models.json support is needs-review on both classes (automated pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets), pending an agent matrix pass to confirm select/slider tap sizes and flip to ok.
Translation runs in the Web Worker; tokens stream to the main thread which only appends text. inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Streaming keeps the UI responsive during decode of the 418M model.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy generation off the main thread and stream progress; keep INP low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — translation runs in the worker; TextStreamer streams the greedy decode so the UI never blocks.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread streaming; inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form labels select menu text input
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control including <select>; keep them keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Source/Target language selects and the source textarea are labelled and keyboard-operable; the selects map directly to the real src_lang/tgt_lang args.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce state via role=status aria-live; consider a lang attribute on translated output for correct pronunciation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: partially applied — status is role=status aria-live and the output is aria-live; a dynamic lang attribute matching the target language on the output region is a recorded follow-up.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: live-status-region + buttons-have-names tested pass; dynamic output lang is an open item

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with wrapping; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — design-system panels; the two language pickers flex-wrap on narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive assertions present; support needs-review pending agent matrix pass

openQuestions: Should the translated output region get a dynamic lang attribute matching the selected target language, so screen readers pronounce it correctly?
Is ~470 MB acceptable to auto-init on mobile, or should the first load carry a more prominent metered-connection warning?

followUpGoals.goal: Set a dynamic lang attribute on the translated output region matching the chosen target language for correct screen-reader pronunciation.
Run the mobile+desktop responsive matrix, confirm/raise select and slider tap targets, and flip models.json support to ok.

summary: Retrospective, code-grounded revision-1 critique for m2m100-translation (Xenova/m2m100_418M, translation, q8, ~470 MB, MIT). Real direct many-to-many translation: worker.js runs the transformers.js pipeline off the main thread with src_lang/tgt_lang picked from two language <select>s (no English pivot), streams tokens via TextStreamer, and reports real token counts + per-token intervals. Auto-init via createModelLoader; role=status aria-live states; no fake output. Verified against the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, loads-clean, declares-*, inference-off-main-thread, shared-auto-init-loader, live-status-region, buttons-have-names tested pass; io-shape/parity/inp manual-evidenced). No fabricated numbers. Honest weaknesses: the translated output region uses the page lang=en rather than a dynamic target-language lang (screen-reader pronunciation); per-token intervals are empty under beam search (disclosed); ~470 MB is a large mobile download; models.json support needs-review pending a tap-target matrix pass.
